situational_context: Early aerobic rebuild — 9 days since bootstrap. First real intensity spike (gravel ride, load 57, Apr 25) successfully absorbed over 3 days. ACWR transiently elevated at 1.44 (above 1.3 threshold), expected to normalize by May 1 as the gravel ride exits the 7d window. Ebike commuting on sur-site days provides consistent Z1 base (~20 load/day). Weight declining steadily (-2.0 kg in 9d, 106.89 → 104.89). RHR dropped 3 bpm week-over-week — positive cardiovascular signal. Training readiness recovered from post-gravel crash (25) to 76. Baby-driven life constraints continue; training revolves around commute days and weekends/holidays. VO2max: 40.9 running, 38.9 cycling.

plan_7d_ahead.type: Sortie longue Z2 — Construction base aérobie

recommendations.text: Let ACWR normalize — keep all sessions Z1-Z2 until May 2. The gravel ride spiked acute load to 1.44× chronic; absorb before adding intensity. Wednesday's Zwift Z1 is a ceiling, not a floor.
Increase daily protein toward 140g+ — currently averaging 119g/d against 150g target (79% adherence). Prioritize post-commute recovery meal and dinner protein bolus.
If Thursday (Apr 30) commute is gravel (Strava suffer score >30), downgrade Friday's Z2 to 40 min Z1 recovery. Ebike commute (suffer <20) means Friday's Z2 stays as planned.
recommendations.measurable: Check Strava suffer score for Apr 30 before committing to May 1 session

markers_to_watch.reason: Currently 1.44 — transient spike from gravel ride (load 57) should exit 7d window by May 2
Lost 2.0 kg in 9 days; if sustained could indicate excessive deficit — need 2 more weeks to distinguish hydration shift from genuine trend

markers_to_watch.threshold: Sustaining below 51 bpm confirms positive adaptation
markers_to_watch.reason: Dropped from 53 to 49.7 — largest weekly shift in 90d baseline; verify it holds through load absorption
